channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Lula, Pedro Almodóvar e outros torcem pelo Oscar para 'O Agente Secreto'; veja
item.description: Brazilian artists and public figures, including President Lula, Anna Muylaert, and Taís Araújo, expressed support for the film 'O Agente Secreto' by Kleber Mendonça Filho on social media ahead of the 2026 Oscar ceremony. Lula emphasized the global recognition of Brazilian culture, while Muylaert highlighted the legacy of Pernambuco cinema and the film as a result of perseverance. The movie is nominated in four categories, including Best Picture and Best Actor for Wagner Moura.

item.title: França: eleições municipais de Paris, Lyon e Marselha serão decididas no 2° turno, apontam estimativas
item.description: The 2026 municipal elections in Paris, Lyon, and Marseille saw significantly low voter turnout, according to early estimates. No candidate secured an absolute majority in any of the three major cities, indicating that runoff elections will be needed to determine the winners. Polling stations closed on Sunday, March 15, with research institutes highlighting a fragmented and polarized electoral landscape.

item.title: Eleitores cansados dos extremos existem; falta quem os lidere para derrotar a polarização
item.description: Voters tired of the polarization between lulism and bolsonarism represent a significant electoral space for moderate candidates, such as PSD governors (Caiado, Ratinho Júnior or Leite), who bet on stability and unity. Despite elections often being defined by emotional identities, international and national examples show that centrist alternatives can win when perceived as competitive and viable. Success depends on political coordination, broad coalition-building, and early signaling of viability to prevent moderate voters from abandoning the option due to perceived risk.

item.title: José Dirceu confirma pré-candidatura a deputado federal por SP
item.description: Former Chief of Staff José Dirceu (PT) confirmed his pre-candidacy for federal deputy from São Paulo in the 2026 elections during a celebration of his 80th birthday on March 15, 2026. He stated the decision was made at the invitation of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva and that he is conducting the pre-campaign within the law and TSE resolutions. Dirceu said he aims to contribute to political articulation in Congress and support the federal government's agenda.

item.title: José Dirceu diz que Tarcísio foi um governador ausente
item.description: Former Minister José Dirceu (PT) criticized Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) for being absent in strategic areas such as scientific and technological development, the state's industrial base, health, education, and public security, claiming crises in the Civil and Military Police. The remarks were made during an event celebrating his 80th birthday in São Paulo, amid discussions of a potential opposition candidacy in 2026.

item.title: Eleições municipais na França têm participação no primeiro turno considerada baixa
item.description: Voter turnout in the first round of France's municipal elections was considered low, with an estimated 57.6% participation rate according to Elabe institute data released after polls closed on Sunday (15). The low turnout may reflect voter disengagement or lack of interest in municipal-level issues.

item.title: Dirceu diz que Haddad tem força para vencer governo de SP
item.description: José Dirceu stated that Fernando Haddad has the electoral strength to win the São Paulo gubernatorial race in 2026, citing his performance in the 2022 runoff with over 45% of the vote. He emphasized there is significant space for an opposition candidacy against Governor Tarcísio de Freitas and mentioned potential running mates such as Márcio França and Marina Silva. The remarks were made during Dirceu’s 80th birthday celebration, which focused on engaging with the Paulista grassroots.

item.title: Extrema-direita avança no sul da França nas eleições municipais
item.description: The far-right leads in the first round of municipal elections in southern France, with National Rally (RN) candidates re-elected in Perpignan and Fréjus and leading in Toulon and Nimes. Low voter turnout (between 56% and 58.5%) reflects political disengagement amid domestic crisis and the Middle East war. The results are seen as a bellwether for the 2027 presidential election, as Jordan Bardella and Marine Le Pen strengthen their positions despite pending judicial ineligibilities.

item.title: Candidato a presidente do Peru morre em acidente de carro
item.description: Peruvian presidential candidate Napoleón Becerra, 61, died in a car crash on Sunday morning (March 15) while en route to a campaign event in Ayacucho. Becerra, the national leader of the Workers and Entrepreneurs Party (PTE) and a centrist political figure, was polling among the lowest in voter intention surveys ahead of the April 12 elections, which featured a record 36 candidates. The National Elections Jury confirmed his death, and a police investigation has been launched to determine the cause of the accident.

item.title: Onde assistir Internacional x Bahia pelo Campeonato Brasileiro neste domingo
item.description: Internacional and Bahia face off this Sunday at 4 p.m. at Beira-Rio Stadium in the sixth round of the 2026 Brasileirão. Internacional aims to secure its first home win to climb the standings, while Bahia looks to sustain its strong start to the season. The match will be broadcast live on Globo and Premiere.

item.title: GP da China: Antonelli se torna o 2º mais jovem a vencer na F1
item.description: Kimi Antonelli of Mercedes won the Chinese Grand Prix on March 15, 2026, at 19 years and 6 months old, becoming the second-youngest driver to win an F1 race, behind only Max Verstappen. His victory ended nearly two decades without an Italian F1 win. Gabriel Bortoleto of Audi failed to start due to hydraulic issues, and both McLaren cars retired due to technical failures.

item.title: Aliados de Flávio organizam ofensiva para buscar eleitores da plataforma social de Lula
item.description: Allies of Flávio Bolsonaro are planning an offensive to attract voters who benefit from federal social programs such as Bolsa Família, using the slogan 'I receive the benefits with my left hand, but vote with my right'. The strategy aims to emphasize that these voters have already secured their right to aid, but now seek a national project that goes beyond mere survival, offering hope for a better future. The assessment is that Lula's main electoral base lies in this broad segment of the population dependent on government transfers.

item.title: Como os brasileiros acham que o governo Lula deve lidar com guerra no Irã, segundo pesquisa
item.description: A Genial/Quaest poll shows 77% of Brazilians support Lula’s government maintaining neutrality in the Iran war, with only 14% favoring support for the U.S. and Israel and 2% for Iran. 81% fear the conflict will spread globally. Conducted March 6–9 with 2,004 respondents, the survey has a 2-point margin of error at 95% confidence.

item.title: Cláusula de barreira fica mais rígida em 2026 e partidos dobram aposta em federações para sobreviver
item.description: The stricter electoral threshold for 2026 is pushing Brazilian parties to form federations as a survival strategy, exemplified by the Renovação Solidária federation (Solidariedade + PRD), which has effectively absorbed four former parties. Alone, these parties would fail to meet the 2.5% national vote or 13 federal deputies across nine states requirement, but united they comply. Experts describe a 'deep reformulation' of the party system, with likely future fusions, including ongoing talks between PSDB and Cidadania, while a proposed merger with Podemos collapsed over leadership disputes.

item.title: Dirigente sindical defende atrair opositores não-bolsonaristas para campanha de Lula
item.description: The general secretary of Força Sindical, João Carlos Gonçalves (Juruna), advocates for Lula's re-election campaign to attract non-bolsonarist opposition figures, such as PSDB leader Aécio Neves and Solidariedade leader Paulinho da Força, to broaden the coalition. He argues these politicians should be 'forgiven' for past actions, similar to Geraldo Alckmin, who voted for Dilma's impeachment in 2016 but now supports Lula. The goal is to strengthen the campaign and safeguard workers' rights and democracy.

item.title: Franceses votam em eleições municipais a um ano das presidenciais
item.description: French voters participated in municipal elections on Sunday, March 15, a vote taking place one year ahead of the 2027 presidential elections. The outcome could see Paris fall under right-wing control and lead to the election of far-right mayors in several cities, signaling a potential political realignment in the country.

item.title: Lula recebe presidente da Bolívia para discutir combate a ilícitos transnacionais
item.description: President Lula (PT) will receive Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz Pereira on Monday, the 16th, at the Palácio do Planalto to discuss cooperation in energy, physical integration, combating transnational illicit activities, trade, investment, development, migration, and consular affairs.

item.title: MG: Influencer e pré-candidato do MBL agride e apanha de gerente de loja
item.description: Influencer and Minas Gerais gubernatorial pre-candidate Ben Mendes of the MBL party physically fought with a store manager in Betim, in the Belo Horizonte metropolitan area, on Friday. Both parties exchanged blows during the altercation, which took place at a commercial establishment.

item.title: Partidos rivais da Hungria fazem grandes comícios antes das eleições de abril
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán and opposition candidate Peter Magyar held large rallies on March 15, Hungary’s national day, ahead of the April 12 elections. Orbán urged supporters to achieve a 'historic victory' amid economic stagnation and rising support for Magyar’s Tisza party, which leads in polls. He framed the vote as a choice between war and peace, accusing the opposition of risking Hungary’s involvement in the Ukraine war, while the opposition warned that another Orbán term would deepen Hungary’s isolation from the EU and threaten its democratic future.

item.title: Tarcísio vai a encontro da ala ortodoxa da Assembleia de Deus
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas attended the 26th Extraordinary General Assembly of the Orthodox Ministers Convention of the Assemblies of God (Comoespo), where he described it as a privilege to be present as a Roman Catholic among evangelical 'brothers'. His appearance reflects an effort to engage with the orthodox wing of the Assemblies of God, a powerful segment within Brazilian evangelicalism.

item.title: Candidato à Presidência do Peru morre em acidente de trânsito
item.description: The Peruvian presidential candidate, Napoleón Becerra of the Workers and Entrepreneurs Party (PTE), died in a traffic accident while en route to a campaign event, as confirmed by local authorities on Sunday, the 15th.

item.title: Flávio diz que centro do Rio parece “cidade fantasma” e associa ao governo Lula
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) criticized the state of Rio de Janeiro's downtown, describing it as a 'ghost town,' and attributed the situation to the Lula government. He made the remarks as a pre-candidate for president, positioning Lula as his main rival in the 2026 elections.

item.title: Com 3 gols de Raphinha, Barcelona goleia o Sevilla e retoma vantagem sobre o Real em La Liga
item.description: Barcelona defeated Sevilla 5-2 at Spotify Camp Nou, with Raphinha scoring three goals (two penalties and one from open play), reclaiming a 4-point lead over Real Madrid in La Liga. The win allowed Hansi Flick to rest Lamine Yamal for the upcoming Champions League second leg against Newcastle, as Barcelona played with offensive fluidity and defensive discipline after their 4-1 loss in the first leg. Cancelo, Dani Olmo, and Fermín stood out, while Yamal and Gavi received warm applause upon their late substitutions.

item.title: Eleição municipal na França testa força da extrema direita antes do pleito presidencial
item.description: The French municipal election held on March 15, 2026, is being closely watched as a test of the far-right's strength, particularly the National Rally (RN), ahead of the 2027 presidential election. The RN aims to gain visibility in cities like Marseille, where its candidate Franck Allisio is tied in polls with incumbent socialist mayor Benoit Payan, amid voter concerns over security and immigration. Results could shape national momentum, especially if the RN secures wins in major urban centers.

item.title: Ciro ‘colocou PT no poder e agora quer apoio do PL’, diz Girão sobre governo do Ceará
item.description: Senator Eduardo Girão (Novo-CE) criticized the potential alliance between the PL and Ciro Gomes (PSDB) for the Ceará gubernatorial race, calling it a 'historical mistake' because Ciro has always identified with the left and helped bring the PT to power in the state. Girão, a pre-candidate for governor, argued that PL's support for Ciro contradicts local right-wing ideology and could serve personal and family interests.

item.title: Eleições municipais na França: participação chega perto de 50% próximo ao fim da votação no 1° turno
item.description: Voter turnout in the first round of France's municipal elections reached 48.90% by 5 p.m. local time on Sunday (15), according to the French Interior Ministry, marking a significant increase from early voting hours. The figure reflects growing voter engagement as polling stations approached closing time.

item.title: Caiado chama Lula de 'embaixador de facções' e defende anistia aos condenados pelo 8/1
item.description: Goiás governor and presidential pré-candidate Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) called President Lula an 'embassador of factions' in a Canal Livre interview, advocating amnesty for those convicted in the January 8 attacks. He accused the federal government of favoring criminal groups and claimed the punishment of January 8 participants is excessive and politically motivated.

item.title: Caiado chama Lula de ‘embaixador de facções’ e defende anistia aos condenados pelo 8/1
item.description: Governor of Goiás and presidential pre-candidate Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) called President Lula an 'ambassador of factions', accusing him of being omissive in fighting organized crime and maintaining an apathetic stance toward drug trafficking. Caiado defended immediate amnesty for those convicted in the January 8, 2023 attacks, stating he would pardon those involved in the attempted coup to 'end this matter' and move forward to work.

item.title: Comícios de Orbán e de opositor reúnem multidões na Hungria
item.description: Tens of thousands attended rival rallies in Budapest on Sunday (March 15, 2026), organized by Prime Minister Viktor Orbán and opposition leader Peter Magyar, amid mutual accusations of foreign interference ahead of the April 12 parliamentary elections. Orbán labeled Magyar a 'puppet' of Brussels and Kyiv and urged Ukraine to stop 'attacking' Hungary, while Magyar accused Orbán of seeking Kremlin support, citing investigations by VSquare and the Financial Times. Both events took place on Hungary’s national holiday commemorating the 1848 Revolution, intensifying political polarization.

item.title: Tarcísio participa de encontro da ala ‘ortodoxa’ da Assembleia de Deus”
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas attended the 26th Extraordinary General Assembly of COMOESPO, linked to the 'orthodox' wing of the Assembly of God, and shared a social media message emphasizing the continuity of Christian faith and the church's role as a light in the world. He thanked the invitation and reaffirmed his commitment to religious values, amid his 2026 re-election bid.

item.title: Muere en un accidente de tráfico un candidato presidencial de Perú a menos de un mes de las elecciones
item.description: Peruvian presidential candidate Napoleón Becerra García died in a traffic accident in the highlands of Ayacucho, less than a month before the general elections. The 61-year-old was en route to a campaign event in the Tambo district when the crash occurred at dawn.

item.title: Flávio diz que Centro do Rio “parece cidade fantasma” e associa situação a Lula
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) criticized the state of Rio de Janeiro's downtown, describing it as a 'ghost town' and linking its economic decline to the government of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. In a social media post, he argued that high taxes, bureaucracy, violence, and lack of hope hinder entrepreneurship and job creation in Brazil, concluding that 'bankruptcy is the mark of the Lula government'.

item.title: Lula recebe presidente da Bolívia após faltar a posse de Kast
item.description: Lula will receive Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz in an official visit on March 16, 2026, after declining to attend José Antonio Kast’s inauguration in Chile. Paz attended the Chilean ceremony and advocated for greater Latin American integration. The visit takes place amid Bolivia’s political transition, with Paz assuming office after the end of MAS hegemony and facing an economic crisis marked by fuel shortages and high inflation.

item.title: Lula, Flávio e o 'Memento Mori'
item.description: The piece reflects on the 'Memento Mori' concept applied to Brazilian politics, highlighting Lula’s presidency as a charismatic and transformative force that drove progress in reducing inequality, expanding education access, and stabilizing the economy—despite corruption scandals and controversial foreign policy alignments. Drawing parallels to historical figures like De Gaulle and Churchill, it underscores his return to power after a hiatus, while referencing Flávio Bolsonaro signals an emerging polarization in the 2026 presidential race.

item.title: Piora na avaliação e palanques rachados viram desafio para Lula no Nordeste
item.description: The Northeast, decisive for Lula's 2022 victory, shows signs of wear: his positive approval dropped from 49% to 41% since September 2023, while rejection rose from 27% to 33% according to Datafolha. Although he still leads with 59% against 30% for Flávio Bolsonaro, fractures in the allied base across at least 6 of the 9 states threaten the PT's dominance in the region, which is essential for his 2026 re-election.

item.title: Lula diz que Oscar reconhece “potência da cultura brasileira”
item.description: President Lula stated that the 2026 Oscars represent international recognition of Brazil's audiovisual strength, highlighting films like 'O Agent Secreto' and referencing national cinema classics. He emphasized Brazil's talent to shine globally and asked followers to choose their favorite Brazilian film.

item.title: Carluxo reclama de “narrativa canalha” sobre dança de Flávio
item.description: Carlos Bolsonaro, former city councilor and son of former President Jair Bolsonaro, defended Senator Flávio Bolsonaro after criticism over a video showing him dancing on a platform during the launch of Senator Marcos Rogério’s pre-candidacy for governor of Rondônia. Carlos labeled the backlash a 'vile narrative,' claiming the gesture was taken out of context and used politically against the Bolsonaro family. The video circulated widely on social media while Jair Bolsonaro remains hospitalized.

item.title: Profissões com as maiores taxas e riscos de divórcio no Brasil
item.description: Professions such as police officers, military personnel, firefighters, healthcare workers on shift, truck drivers, and entertainment industry workers show the highest divorce rates in Brazil due to unpredictable schedules, high stress, frequent travel, and intense social interaction, which strain time, emotional energy, and communication in marriage.

item.title: A ‘bíblia’ econômica da campanha de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: The new book by Paulo Guedes, 'The Path to Prosperity', will serve as the economic bible for Flávio Bolsonaro's 2026 campaign. The over-800-page work, co-authored by former members of Bolsonaro's economic team, details policies from 2019–2022 and includes the ex-minister's inauguration speech with current data updates.

item.title: PT 'torce' por candidatura de Kim ao Governo de SP para viabilizar segundo turno
item.description: PT leaders are discreetly rooting for Kim Kataguiri's gubernatorial candidacy in São Paulo, hoping his presence in a runoff would split the right-wing vote and help Fernando Haddad defeat Tarcísio de Freitas. The strategy aims to prevent a first-round victory by the current governor. The report was published on March 15, 2026.

item.title: Flávio rebate críticas por animação em evento: ‘Fazendo o que meu pai pediu’
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro defended himself against criticism for appearing cheerful and energetic at a political event in Rondônia, stating he was following his father Jair Bolsonaro’s request to bring hope to the Brazilian people. He said he went directly to the hospital afterward to inform his father about the successful launch of the pre-candidacies of Marcos Rogério, Fernando Máximo, and Bruno Sheidt, noting that Jair Bolsonaro was pleased with the news. Despite the ex-president’s ongoing hospitalization for bilateral bacterial pneumonia in the ICU, Flávio framed his attitude as an act of obedience and optimism.

item.title: Flávio tenta se explicar após dança em palanque?
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) posted a video on Sunday morning, the 15th, in which he attempts to explain himself after being criticized for dancing atop a platform during the event launching Senator Marcos Rogério’s (PL-RO) pre-candidacy for governor of Rondônia. The recording went viral on social media, sparking controversy as many viewed the act as inappropriate in a serious political context, especially given the implied presence of former President Jair Bolsonaro. Flávio seeks to mitigate reputational damage, claiming the moment was lighthearted and not intended to disrespect the event.

item.title: Sem observar Neymar, Ancelotti convoca para últimos testes antes da lista final
item.description: Carlo Ancelotti will reveal this Monday (16) the final call-up for the friendlies against France and Croatia, which will serve as last tests before the World Cup 2026 roster is set. Neymar, despite 12 days of preparation, did not participate in tactical training against Mirassol due to muscle fatigue and was rested, sparking criticism on social media. The Italian coach faces ongoing pressure regarding Neymar’s inclusion, as the player has yet to play under his leadership.

item.title: Seleção brasileira feminina derrota Mali e permanece com chance no Pré-Mundial de basquete
item.description: The Brazilian women's basketball team defeated Mali 76-73 in Wuhan, rallying in the final quarter to keep alive their hopes of qualifying for the 2026 FIBA World Cup in Germany. Brazil must now beat China on Tuesday or rely on other results to secure one of the three available spots. Kamilla Cardoso led Brazil with 17 points and 11 rebounds, while Bella Nascimento scored all 12 of her points in the final period to spark the comeback.

item.title: França registra participação abaixo de 20% nas horas iniciais do 1° turno das eleições municipais
item.description: Voter turnout in France's municipal elections first round was below 20% in the initial hours, nearing the 2020 record low of 18.38%. Nearly 49 million voters were called to the polls on Sunday, but the weak participation reflects a broader trend of electoral disengagement in recent French local elections.

item.title: Um cenário insólito para a indicação de Jorge Messias ao Supremo
item.description: President Lula nominated Attorney General Jorge Messias for the Supreme Federal Court in November, but the appointment remains stalled due to a political impasse with Senator Davi Alcolumbre, who controls the Senate’s agenda. Alcolumbre may delay the vote until after the October elections to negotiate with the next president, echoing his past obstruction of Bolsonaro’s nomination of André Mendonça. Messias has sought senatorial support without success, while Lula is leveraging the promise of a future STF nomination for Rodrigo Pacheco to encourage him to run for governor of Minas Gerais.

item.title: Defesa aguarda novo laudo para pedir prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro, diz Flávio
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated that the defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro is awaiting a new medical report to request house arrest again. The comment was made following a visit to his father, who is hospitalized at DF Star Hospital in Brasília.

item.title: STF avança em investigações contra Lulinha e políticos que desviaram emendas
item.description: The STF is accelerating investigations against politicians, including Lula's son (Lulinha) and the INSS Careca, with evidence of influence peddling. Linked to the Banco Master scandal, the probe aims to imprison those responsible this semester, avoiding interference in the 2026 electoral campaign.

item.title: ‘Alcolumbre precisa pedir para sair’, diz senador Eduardo Girão
item.description: Senator Eduardo Girão (Novo-CE) urged Senate President Davi Alcolumbre (União Brasil-AP) to resign for obstructing the installation of a parliamentary inquiry into the Banco Master scandal, despite having 51 signatures. He also renewed calls for the impeachment of Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes, citing contracts between Moraes' wife's law firm and the bank, and alleged messages with the bank's owner, arguing the scandal has broken through right-wing echo chambers and intensified pressure on the Court.

item.title: Temer participa de debate sobre panorama eleitoral na Fiesp
item.description: Former Brazilian president Michel Temer (MDB) will participate in a debate at Fiesp on the Brazilian electoral outlook on March 16, 2026, alongside writer Denis Rosenfield and political scientist Rubens Figueiredo. The event will discuss election prospects, with Temer's allies encouraging him to run again for president as a way to reduce polarization between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro.
